calendar.matters: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration
Off Calendar May-26-2026 Continued To Aug-25-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Apr-30-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jon R. Vaught for Adelaida Salangsang, Petitioner (via Zoom).
Janet Boessenecker for SF Public Administrator (via Zoom).
Richard Van Koll, son of decedent (via Zoom).

Mr. Vaught reported that the Public Administrator had declined to act after prior continuances and that, as a creditor, Ms. Salangsang was statutorily eligible for appointment.

Mr. Van Koll appeared and objected to the petition, asserting that no probate was necessary because the decedent established a valid and irrevocable trust. He stated that he and his brothers were named trustees and that the petitioner and her husband had already initiated separate legal proceedings against them involving the trust. Mr. Van Koll rejected the creditor's claim, alleging it was fraudulent and under investigation.

Mr. Vaught responded that California law requires a probate estate to be opened in order to pursue a creditor's claim, and that his client, as a creditor, had priority to file the petition. He noted the absence of any competing petition by Mr. Van Koll despite his awareness of the matter for at least nine months.

Ms. Boessenecker confirmed the SF Public Administrator had declined to act, saw no reason to be involved in the pending litigation, and acknowledged the petitioner's priority under probate statutes. Ms. Boessenecker also noted that the roles of trustee and estate administrator are distinct, and that Mr. Van Koehl's position appeared to conflate the two.

After discussion, the court took the petition filed June 18, 2024 under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)
